preamble: The shift from a diphthong [ai] to a high nasalized vowel [ĩ] involves both nasalization and vowel raising, often occurring in specific phonetic contexts or as part of a vowel reduction process.

examples.note: Hypothetical or dialectal shift involving nasalization and raising in specific linguistic environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
